repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

body:
Problem: when resize events from DEC private mode 2048 are enabled, the TUI ignores all SIGWINCH signals. This causes SIGWINCH-triggered redraws to be ignored even if the terminal size hasn't changed.

While we already had a test for it, it currently runs under libvterm, which doesn't enable that mode for the child TUI.

Solution: only ignore SIGWINCH events while resize events are enabled if the new terminal size has changed, and make the current test explicitely enable mode 2048 so that it always hits the code path we care about.

comment_data.body:
confusing that this is named "get" now. it's still the same thing. add an underscore if needed.

```suggestion
static void _tui_guess_size(TUIData *tui, int *width, int *height)
```

```suggestion
    -- Exercise the tui.c resize-events path (resize_events_enabled=true) by feeding DECRPM "mode
    -- 2048 currently reset" report. This makes the child TUI enable resize events.
```
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -2790,6 +2790,11 @@ describe('TUI', function()
     -- On Windows, SIGWINCH cannot be sent as a signal with uv_kill(), while
     -- SIGWINCH handlers are only called on terminal resize.
     t.skip(is_os('win'), 'N/A for Windows')
+    -- Exercise the resize-events path even when the terminal emulator used by
+    -- this test does not support DEC private mode 2048. A reset mode report
+    -- makes the child TUI enable resize events.

comment_data.body: why call this again after `tui_get_size` was (possibly) called just above?

comment_regular_data.body:
> This causes SIGWINCH-triggered redraws to be ignored even if the terminal size hasn't changed.

But why is this a problem?

The whole point of using resize events is so that we don’t have to issue a bunch of ioctls in every SIGWINCH callback. This PR adds those back in. So we may as well just remove resize events altogether.

are terminals who use size reporting generally disabling sending SIGWINCH while this mode is active? otherwise this will still cause double redraws on every resize (timer throttling could be an option, but that risks becoming icky and complicated)

> are terminals who use size reporting generally disabling sending SIGWINCH while this mode is active? otherwise this will still cause double redraws on every resize (timer throttling could be an option, but that risks becoming icky and complicated)

AFAIK terminals do not stop sending SIGWINCH when the resize events mode is active. It is still received by the application, we can just choose to ignore them.

Is the point of this PR to preserve the use-case of https://github.com/neovim/neovim/issues/23411 ? Or is there some other point?

If it's only for https://github.com/neovim/neovim/issues/23411 , then maybe we can make it opt-in, idk.

> is there some other point?

No, it's only to avoid regressing https://github.com/neovim/neovim/issues/23411 (and tbh I'm starting to wonder whether that's a good-enough reason to make this code path more complex than it needs to be).

> maybe we can make it opt-in

How? abduco sets [these](https://github.com/martanne/abduco/blob/8c32909a159aaa9484c82b71f05b7a73321eb491/abduco.1#L148-L151) env vars, so maybe we could detect them and only check the size if they're both set?
Nvim now has `:detach` / `--remote-ui` /  `:connect` .  So I think we should regress https://github.com/neovim/neovim/issues/23411 (until a simple solution is found), WDYT @bfredl @zeertzjq

title: fix(tui): redraw on SIGWINCH when resize events are enabled
